message:
🐛 Fix GA4 geolocation for localhost installs (#1531)

The analytics proxy was setting _uip=127.0.0.1 for localhost users,
which overrides GA4's natural IP detection with a non-routable address.
GA4 cannot geolocate loopback/private IPs, causing the map widget to
show no location dots.

Fix: Skip setting _uip and X-Forwarded-For when the detected client IP
is private (loopback, RFC-1918, link-local). For localhost deployments,
the proxy's outbound connection already originates from the user's real
public IP, so GA4 geolocates correctly without the override.

For production (behind a load balancer), X-Forwarded-For contains the
real public IP, which continues to be forwarded as before.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
